_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to execute OS command line (e.g., ipconfig, netstat, dir, nbtstat).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-008 (TF, cosine: 0.446), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.400).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.3/10 (raw 0.333). Shared vocabulary: [command, line]. DCWF-only terms [ability, dir, execute, ipconfig, nbtstat, netstat] vs WIDAI-only terms [common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals, knowledge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.845 (P=0.846, R=0.844). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 32-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.1%). "Ability to execute OS command line (e.g., ipconfig, netstat, dir, nbtstat)." and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.471 > STS 0.333 (gap +0.138). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [command, line] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.1%. Shared tokens: [command, line]. DCWF-only: [ability, dir, execute, ipconfig, nbtstat, netstat]. WIDAI-only: [common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals, knowledge].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.138 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
